retry_reason: On send failure, the function releases the reference (put) and returns an error. Retrying with the same skb would likely fail again, and the caller would need to allocate a new skb and possibly a new wr_waitp object; the function is not a simple get/put wrapper where retry makes sense.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to send an offload message (c4iw_ofld_send) and wait for a reply (c4iw_wait_for_reply). Although it includes c4iw_get_wr_wait and c4iw_put_wr_wait calls, these are merely supporting the send-and-wait operation — the core logic is not refcount management. The refcount operations are conditional on the send success/failure and are incidental to the overall communication sequence.
judge.retry_reason: On send failure, the function releases the reference (put) and returns an error.
